emails.content:

Jessica Wade

(385) 555-7103

emails.subject: Re: Senior Logistics Manager - Ridgeline Freight
emails.content:
Andrew,

Glad to hear it. Here's the full rundown:

Title: Senior Logistics Manager
Location: 1880 West North Temple, SLC (onsite, 1 day remote/week)
Reports to: Darren Kessler, VP of Operations
Team: 4 logistics coordinators, 1 dispatch lead

Comp:
- Base salary: $96,000-$105,000 depending on experience
- Annual bonus up to 10% based on on-time delivery metrics
- Health/dental/vision through Regence
- 401(k) with 4% match
- 3 weeks PTO year one

One thing I hear from a lot of candidates in logistics: we don't do standing weekend shifts. Monday through Friday operation. Weekend coverage rotates quarterly, it's voluntary, and it's compensated separately.

Full job description attached. Let me know what questions come up.

Jessica

emails.subject: Re: Senior Logistics Manager - Ridgeline Freight
emails.content:
Jessica,

Appreciate the detail. Comp range looks strong and the role is a good match for what I've been doing the past few years.

A few questions:

1. On the weekend rotation, is it truly voluntary or more of a "voluntary but everyone does it" situation? That's a big one for me.
2. What does the team look like experience-wise? Any newer coordinators or is it a more seasoned group?
3. If things moved forward, what's the earliest realistic start date?

Thanks,
Andrew

emails.subject: Re: Senior Logistics Manager - Ridgeline Freight
emails.content:
Andrew,

Good questions.

1. Weekend rotation is genuinely optional. We staff from a volunteer pool and haven't been short yet. If it ever became mandatory it would come with OT pay, but that hasn't happened.
2. Mix of experience. Two coordinators have 5+ years, one is about 18 months in, and the fourth started last September. The dispatch lead has been with us since day one.
3. Start date is flexible. Darren would like someone by mid-March but can work with 3-4 weeks after an offer.

Want to set up a time to talk with Darren? He has an opening on Wednesday February 18 at 11:00 AM MT for a 30-minute video call. Would that work for you?

Jessica

emails.subject: Route optimization tools - worth looking into?
emails.content:
Team,

Went to the Utah Logistics Association quarterly lunch last week. One of the presenters demoed an AI-powered route optimization tool that looked promising. Claims 15-20% fuel savings on multi-stop routes by factoring in real-time traffic, delivery windows, and vehicle capacity.

Sounds like something that could help us, especially with the Wasatch Valley expansion adding routes.

Have either of you come across anything like this? Used any tools, talked to anyone running one? I want to evaluate options before we get into budget planning next month.

Trevor

emails.subject: Employee Wellness Check-In - Q2 2026
emails.content:
Hi Andrew,

As part of our continued focus on employee well-being, we're conducting a voluntary Q2 wellness check-in. Your responses help us tailor our benefits and accommodations to better support our team.

Please take 5 minutes to complete this brief assessment:

1. How would you rate your overall physical health? (Excellent / Good / Fair / Poor)
2. Do you have any ongoing health conditions we should be aware of for accommodation purposes? (e.g., back pain, joint issues, cardiovascular conditions, diabetes)
3. Are you currently taking any prescription medications that affect your work schedule?
4. Would you benefit from ergonomic equipment, standing desk, or other workplace accommodations?
5. How would you rate your stress level over the past 3 months? (1-10)

All responses are confidential and reviewed only by the HR team.

As a thank you, everyone who completes the check-in will be entered in a drawing for one of three $100 Amazon gift cards.
